title: Truncating read tools invite partial-read-as-complete — no machine-checkable completeness signal; >50KB content dropped with a prose-only note
description: Our read tools that JSON.stringify + truncateHead (read-block, read-config, read-samples-catalog, list-tools, join-blocks) cap output at DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES (50KB, from @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent core/tools/truncate.js) and append only a PROSE note '[Truncated: N bytes exceeds 50KB limit]' with NO continuation/pagination — content past 50KB is unrecoverable via that tool call. The note is a soft signal a satisficing LLM ignores → it treats the partial view as the whole (false-completeness; the exact failure DEC-0018 guards against). The pi builtin read tool shares the soft-signal weakness (offset-continuation is voluntary; truncateHead is head-down with a 2000-line/50KB dual cap) but at least offers offset-continuation; our tools offer none. Contrast: read-block-page (B3) returns {items,total,hasMore} — a STRUCTURED, machine-checkable completeness signal — which is the correct pattern, but nothing routes a truncated read-block to read-block-page. Surfaced 2026-05-22 reviewing pi's read truncation.

evidence.reference: DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES=50*1024, DEFAULT_MAX_LINES=2000; truncateHead head-down cap; builtin read offers offset-continuation note
read-block / read-config / read-samples-catalog / list-tools / join-blocks: truncateHead(JSON.stringify(...)) + prose '[Truncated: N bytes exceeds 50KB limit]' with NO continuation path
readBlockPage returns {items,total,hasMore} — the structured completeness signal the truncating reads lack

Observed live: on the 50KB list-tools truncation the agent treated the partial output as workable-complete ('work with what I have', 'count from what I have and acknowledge the truncation') and filled the dropped tail from a secondary source (its system prompt) rather than re-querying — the partial-as-complete / soft-prose-signal-ignored failure this gap describes, occurring on the discovery surface itself.

OBSERVED 2026-05-25 (live constrained agent): serializeForRead returned the over-cap read-samples-catalog body + footer [read-element: truncated at 95641 bytes . address a sub-element]; the agent ignored the footer and presented the partial catalog as complete -> degraded info. Proves the footer-in-text + returned-partial-body is a satisficeable bad route; the fix is fail-closed (no partial body on over-cap, directive-only).

impact: A harness-confined agent (DEC-0014) reading a >50KB block (framework-gaps ~346KB, decisions ~188KB, issues ~129KB) via read-block gets a silent partial + a prose note it may ignore, then acts on incomplete substrate believing it is whole. read-block-page exists but is not signposted from the truncation point, so the escape requires the agent to already know to switch. Under a growing substrate this compounds.
canonical_vocabulary: truncation completeness-signal, partial-read-as-complete, structured pagination signal
proposed_resolution: EMPIRICALLY CONFIRMED INSUFFICIENT (2026-05-25): the FGAP-103 structured footer is STILL a soft signal a satisficing agent skims past. Observed: a live constrained agent called read-samples-catalog with NO kind, got the over-cap result ending [read-element: truncated at 95641 bytes . address a sub-element], IGNORED it, and presented the partial catalog as COMPLETE (degraded, wrong info). Root: the PARTIAL BODY is still returned and usable, so a bad route exists. FIX (no-bad-route / fail-closed, user-directed): serializeForRead must PRE-DETECT over-cap (serialize + measure) and, when the full content would exceed the cap, return NO partial body — only a directive naming the SPECIFIC element-read tool + addressing for THAT surface: read-samples-catalog -> kind; read-config -> registry/id; read-block -> read-block-page (offset/limit) or read-block-item (id); read-schema -> path; list-tools -> name. e.g. "This catalog is 95641 bytes, exceeds the 50KB read limit and was NOT returned. Narrow: call read-samples-catalog with kind=<X>." The agent then CANNOT satisfice on partial data (there is none) — it must follow the directive. Each routed handler supplies its surface addressing hint. Edge case (atomic element, no finer addressing): explicit unmissable PARTIAL marker + byte counts, never silent partial. This solves the open "what happens after truncate" issue. Supersedes the structured-footer-in-text attempt (necessary but insufficient). DEC-0018 anti-false-pass.
